title: cordylanthus maritimus var. parryi
intro: Cordylanthus maritimus var. parryi, also known as Parry's cordgrass, is a subspecies of the Cordylanthus maritimus plant, a rare species of flowering plant in the figwort family native to the coastal dunes and scrub of western North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Cordylanthus maritimus var. parryi include its potential to support the immune system and reduce the risk of infection, as well as its ability to aid in the recovery from illness and injury. Herbal preparations of Cordylanthus maritimus var. parryi typically involve drying the leaves and flowers of the plant and infusing them in hot water to make a tea, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
